title: Highest Paid Employee
description: Write a query to find the employee with the highest salary.

examples.explanation: Bob has the highest salary of 70000. We order the employees by salary in descending order and take the first result using LIMIT 1.

solution: SELECT name, salary FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 1;

test_cases.setup_sql: CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, name TEXT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Alice',50000),(2,'Bob',70000),(3,'Charlie',60000);

hidden_test_cases.setup_sql: CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, name TEXT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Diana',82000),(2,'Evan',76000),(3,'Farah',91000);
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, name TEXT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Solo',45000);
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, name TEXT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Neo',-1000),(2,'Mia',0),(3,'Zara',125000),(4,'Ian',124999);
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, name TEXT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'A',NULL),(2,'B',30000),(3,'C',99999);
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, name TEXT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Rita',47000),(2,'Sam',46000),(3,'Tina',47001),(4,'Uma',46999),(5,'Vik',-5);

title: Average Salary
description: Find the average salary of all employees.

examples.explanation: The average salary is calculated by summing all salaries (50000 + 70000 + 60000 = 180000) and dividing by the number of employees (3). 180000 / 3 = 60000.

solution: SELECT AVG(salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees;

test_cases.setup_sql: CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,50000),(2,70000),(3,60000);

hidden_test_cases.setup_sql: CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,40000),(2,60000),(3,80000),(4,100000);
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,55000),(2,55000);
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,NULL),(2,40000),(3,80000),(4,NULL);
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, salary INT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,-5000),(2,5000),(3,0);
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, salary INT);

title: Count Products
description: Count total number of products available.

examples.explanation: There are 3 products in the products table: Phone, Laptop, and Tablet. The COUNT(*) function counts all rows in the table.

solution: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_products FROM products;

test_cases.setup_sql: CREATE TABLE products(id INT, name TEXT); INSERT INTO products VALUES (1,'Phone'),(2,'Laptop'),(3,'Tablet');

hidden_test_cases.setup_sql: CREATE TABLE products(id INT, name TEXT); INSERT INTO products VALUES (1,'Mouse'),(2,'Keyboard'),(3,'Monitor'),(4,'Desk');
CREATE TABLE products(id INT, name TEXT); INSERT INTO products VALUES (1,'Single');
CREATE TABLE products(id INT, name TEXT); INSERT INTO products VALUES (1,'Cable'),(1,'Cable-Pro'),(2,NULL),(3,'');
CREATE TABLE products(id INT, name TEXT);
CREATE TABLE products(id INT, name TEXT); INSERT INTO products VALUES (10,'Phone'),(20,'Laptop'),(30,'Laptop'),(40,'Tablet'),(50,'Accessory');

title: Orders Above 100
description: Find orders where amount is greater than 100.

examples.explanation: Only orders with id 2 (amount 200) and id 3 (amount 150) have amounts greater than 100. Order id 1 with amount 50 is excluded because 50 is not greater than 100.

solution: SELECT id, amount FROM orders WHERE amount > 100;

test_cases.setup_sql: CREATE TABLE orders(id INT, amount INT); INSERT INTO orders VALUES (1,50),(2,200),(3,150);

hidden_test_cases.setup_sql: CREATE TABLE orders(id INT, amount INT); INSERT INTO orders VALUES (1,99),(2,100),(3,101),(4,250);
CREATE TABLE orders(id INT, amount INT); INSERT INTO orders VALUES (1,20),(2,40);
CREATE TABLE orders(id INT, amount INT); INSERT INTO orders VALUES (1,100),(2,101),(3,102),(4,NULL),(5,-2);
CREATE TABLE orders(id INT, amount INT); INSERT INTO orders VALUES (10,1000),(20,150),(30,101),(40,100);
CREATE TABLE orders(id INT, amount INT); INSERT INTO orders VALUES (5,100),(6,100),(7,101);

title: Department Employee Count
description: Find total employees in each department.

examples.explanation: The IT department has 2 employees (employee id 2 and 3), and the HR department has 1 employee (employee id 1). We use GROUP BY to group employees by their department and COUNT(*) to count how many employees are in each group.

solution: SELECT department, COUNT(*) AS total FROM employees GROUP BY department;

test_cases.setup_sql: CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, department TEXT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'HR'),(2,'IT'),(3,'IT');

hidden_test_cases.setup_sql: CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, department TEXT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'HR'),(2,'IT'),(3,'IT'),(4,'Sales'),(5,'Sales'),(6,'Sales');
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, department TEXT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Ops');
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, department TEXT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'IT'),(2,'IT'),(3,NULL),(4,NULL),(5,'HR');
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, department TEXT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Sales'),(2,'sales'),(3,'Sales');
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, department TEXT); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,''),(2,''),(3,'Ops');
